name: Russell Jacquet
abstract: Russell Jacquet (December 4, 1917 – February 28, 1990) was an American trumpeter.

concerts.label: Fred Robbins One-Nite Stand: Illinois Jacquet and His Orchestra with Ella Fitzgerald
Kool Jazz Festival: Benny Carter and Illinois Jacquet

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Thompson
about_quotes.evidence: I collaborated with him in playing and arranging and writing some music

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I remember when J. J. came there with Illinois Jacquet. It was Illinois Jacquet, Russell Jacquet, Leo Parker on baritone saxophone, J. J. Johnson on trombone, and a rhythm section. We would stand out in the back where we could hear them from behind and listen.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Jacquet was so big, he needed an opening act to bring – to start the show, to set it up for him to be the finale. He chose the George Hudson band... We had in this band a little tenor [saxophone] player by the name of Willie Parker... Jacquet brought us back
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I had done quite a bit of research and practicing... I had done so many recordings with Jacquet Jacquet and Dizzy also... with Illinois Jacquet Jacquet, and his orchestra. And in that band was... Russell Jacquet

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him, Illinois Jacquet and his brother, Russell Jacquet
